text:
Vīrudha-Āhāra Māraṇa – The Energetic Food Poison Curse
This is one of the least visible yet most insidious forms of Māraṇa. It operates through
energetic poisoning of the victim’s food stream, often by ritualistically corrupting the
prāṇa of what the victim consumes—physically or mentally.
Used in Kaula, Vāma, and Bhūta Tantra, this method doesn’t require direct contact, but
uses food (or its energetic twin) as a carrier of destruction.
🔮 I. Prerequisites & Conditions
🛑 Ritual Conditions:
Must be performed during Krishna Pakṣa, especially Chaturdaśī, Amāvasyā, or during
Grahaṇa (eclipse).
Day: Tuesday or Saturday
Location: Kitchen shrine, cremation field, or near an anthill or termite mound (symbol of
digestive decay)
Fasting for 1 day before the ritual is recommended

n: Kitchen shrine, cremation field, or near an anthill or termite mound (symbol of
digestive decay)
Fasting for 1 day before the ritual is recommended

🪄 II. Ingredients & Materials
Material Purpose
Victim’s food remnants / plate DNA & Prāṇa link
Old or spoiled grain/flour Symbolic corruption of Anna Maya Kosha
Cow dung ash or cremation ash Embodiment of mrityu tattva
Dried chillies + black mustard Digestive irritation & heat
Garlic skin, onion skin Tamasic binding agents
Crushed bitter neem leaves Disease invocation
Oil used for corpse bathing (if accessible) Death resonance
Black turmeric + Triphala powder Tridoshic imbalance triggers
Bone fragment of bird/rat/cat Carries roaming spirit energy (bhūta infuser)
🧪 III. Pre-Ritual Energy Activation
Prepare a Bhūta Bhakṣaṇa Piṇḍa (spirit food ball):
Mix spoiled grains, neem powder, mustard, garlic skin, and cow dung ash into a dough
ball.
On this dough ball, inscribe: “अअअअअ अअअअ अअअअ” (Let this food become poison).
Dry under moonlight for 3 nights while reciting:

ash into a dough
ball.
On this dough ball, inscribe: “अअअअअ अअअअ अअअअ” (Let this food become poison).
Dry under moonlight for 3 nights while reciting:

अ अअअअअअअ अअअअअअअअअ अअअअअअअअअ अअअअअअअअअअअअ अअअअअ
अअअअअअअअ अअअअ अअअअअअअ
(108× each night)
🔧 IV. Ritual Execution
🍽 Step 1: Remote Infusion via Victim’s Plate or Name
If you have access to the victim’s used plate, utensils, or photo:
Place the plate on a black cloth with a red triangle drawn on it.
Put the dried dough-ball on the plate.
Chant this mantra:
अ अअअअअ अअअअअ अअअ अअअअअअअअअअअ अअअअअ अअअअ अअअअ अअअअअअअ
(Repeat 108 times)
With every 9th chant, sprinkle triphala + black turmeric powder onto the plate.

☠️ Step 2: Bhūta Infusion
Burn incense made of dried rat bones, chillies, and mustard.
Face South and invoke: “अअअअअअअअअ अअअअअअअअ, अअअ अअअअअ अअअअ
अअअअअअअ”
Leave the cursed ball overnight under a thorny tree or bury it near a marketplace where
food is sold.
💀 V. Effects on Victim
The food poisoning doesn’t show up medically but causes:
Sudden loss of appetite or uncontrollable overeating
Diseases like ulcers, IBS, Crohn’s, gallbladder stones
Hallucinations of being “fed filth,” nausea after rituals
Aversion to prasad or sattvic food

ng
Diseases like ulcers, IBS, Crohn’s, gallbladder stones
Hallucinations of being “fed filth,” nausea after rituals
Aversion to prasad or sattvic food

Fatigue, emotional dullness, or detachment
🛡 VI. Countermeasures
Method Action
Anna Shuddhi Vidhi Food cleansing with Ganga water + tulsi + ghee lamp daily
Sudarśana Homa Fire ritual to burn food karma
Bagalāmukhī Jīvana Kavacha Protective mantra to neutralize digestive curse
Daily Ajwain + Rock Salt + Camphor Smoke Natural neutralizer for digestive māraṇa
📜 Summary Table: Vīrudha-Āhāra Māraṇa
Aspect Details
Type Energetic Food Curse
Tools Used Spirit-infused food items, victim’s utensils, mantras
Symptoms Gut decay, food aversion, spiritual nausea, dullness
Targets Anna Maya Kosha, digestive fire, mood and mind
Reversal Methods Sudarshana Homa, Bagala mantra, Ajwain rituals

Risk to User Recoil if food consumed by unintended person
Dharmic Status Extremely discouraged unless used for defense
Initiation Required? Yes, Kaula / Vāma / Aghora lineage
